name: Kenneth Walker
headline: Explosive but inefficient back lands in a new backfield
body:
Walker's tape screams big-play juice and screams volatility in equal measure. In 2025 he ripped off a 14.93% explosive-run rate with 33 carries of 10-plus yards, 10 of 20-plus, and a 55-yard touchdown, while forcing 23 broken tackles. Yet the efficiency lagged: 4.65 yards per carry papered over a minus-0.029 EPA per play, just 1.84 yards after contact per attempt, and 26 carries stuffed for negative yardage. He finished RB17 on 160.9 points across 17 games.

The receiving role stayed thin at 36 targets and a 7.9% target share, and his red-zone equity shrank to eight goal-line carries and five rushing scores after eight in 2023. A move off Seattle resets his outlook entirely: the breakaway speed travels, but the snap rate (46.9%) and contact balance need a friendlier scheme to push him back toward a weekly RB1 ceiling.
